title: Stable Merge Sort with Incorrect Edge Handling

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The input consists of an array of integers. The array will contain between 1 and 10000 elements, inclusive. Each element will be a 32-bit integer.
statement.outputFormat: Return true if the input array is sorted in non-ascending order, and false otherwise.
statement.notes: Your task is to fix the buggy merge sort implementation to handle edge cases correctly.

examples.explanation: The array [5, 2, 8, 3] is not sorted in non-ascending order, so the function should return False.
The array [1] is already sorted in non-ascending order, so the function should return True.

hints.text: Consider the merge function and think about how it handles edge cases.

editorial.title: Editorial for Stable Merge Sort with Incorrect Edge Handling
